description: Five-phase LP intelligence pipeline covering GP evaluation, fund terms analysis, portfolio monitoring (LP lens), manager due diligence, and re-up decision. Coordinates 11 specialist agents plus 3 general-purpose agents across sequential phases to produce a terminal verdict of RE-UP, REDUCE, or EXIT. Inverts the standard CRE pipeline perspective: where GP-side orchestrators manage assets, this orchestrator protects LP capital. Phase 3 (Portfolio Monitoring) is recurring quarterly. Cross-chain handoffs: inbound from fund-management (fundNAV, feeSchedule, distributionHistory, portfolioComposition) and portfolio-management (allocationMatrix, concentrationAnalysis); outbound to fund-management (reUpDecision, allocationSize, termsFeedback). Terminal deliverable is an IC-grade re-up recommendation memo with conviction scoring, commitment sizing, and negotiation points.

inputs: config/deal.json
GP marketing materials and PPMs
prior fund performance data (net IRR, DPI, TVPI by vintage)
deal-level MOIC data (realized and unrealized)
capital call and distribution cash flow history
subscription credit facility disclosure
vintage benchmark data (NCREIF, Cambridge Associates, Preqin)
outputs: per-fund net IRR decomposition with sub-line adjustment
DPI trajectory analysis vs vintage median
loss ratio analysis (capital destruction rate by fund)
return dispersion analysis (Gini coefficient, top-deal concentration)
persistence assessment (transition matrix, persistence score)
track record verdict (1-5 rating)

validationRules.description: Net IRR must be computed for each prior fund, or explicitly flagged as data gap.
Each fund must be benchmarked against vintage peers with percentile ranking.
Persistence score must be computed if GP has 2+ prior funds with realized returns.
Track record verdict must be one of EXCEPTIONAL, STRONG, ACCEPTABLE, WEAK, UNPROVEN.

inputs: config/deal.json
LPA fee provisions (management fee, carry, offsets)
GP financial statements (fee revenue, transaction fees)
prior fund fee data (historical gross-to-net spread)
peer fund fee benchmarks by strategy and vintage
outputs: management fee drag analysis (investment period + harvest period)
carry accrual projection under bull/base/bear scenarios
hidden fee inventory (organizational, transaction, monitoring, related-party)
gross-to-net bridge (step-by-step from gross return to LP net)
fee benchmarking vs strategy-matched peers
total fee load rating (LP-Favorable / At Market / Above Market / Excessive)

validationRules.description: Gross-to-net bridge must show every fee layer from gross IRR to LP net IRR.
Carry must be modeled under at least 3 return scenarios (bull, base, bear).
Total fee load must be benchmarked against strategy-matched peer data.
Total fee load rating must be assigned from the defined classification.

inputs: config/deal.json
LPA or term sheet (full document)
ILPA Model LPA provisions
market terms benchmarks by strategy, fund size, and vintage
prior fund LPA terms (for evolution analysis)
outputs: provision-by-provision terms comparison matrix
terms score card (economic, governance, constraints, transparency)
financial impact of terms deviations (dollar and bps)
negotiation priority list (top 5 provisions with target terms)
missing provisions inventory

validationRules.description: Management fee, carry, preferred return, GP co-invest, and offsets must all be analyzed.
Key man, no-fault removal, LPAC, LP consent rights, and reporting must all be analyzed.
Weighted terms score must be computed from all 4 dimensions.
Negotiation priority list must contain at least 3 items with target terms.

inputs: config/deal.json
LPA waterfall provisions (tiers, rates, catch-up, clawback)
fund parameters (committed capital, fund term, fee structure)
deployment profile assumptions
exit profile assumptions
outputs: waterfall scenario analysis (6 return scenarios with LP/GP economics)
European vs American waterfall comparison on identical cash flows
catch-up sensitivity analysis (no catch-up / 50-50 / full)
carry breakpoint table (gross IRR levels where carry becomes material)
GP economics asymmetry analysis (total GP take as % of gross at each return level)

validationRules.description: At least 4 return scenarios must be modeled with LP and GP economics.
European vs American comparison must be produced if fund uses American waterfall.
Carry breakpoint analysis must identify the gross IRR at which carry exceeds 20% and 30% of profits.
Total LP distributions + total GP carry must equal total fund distributions within 0.1%.

inputs: GP quarterly report and capital account statement
LP cash flow data (capital calls and distributions by date)
GP-reported metrics (NAV, TVPI, DPI, RVPI, net IRR)
deal-level performance data (if available)
prior quarter verification results (for trend analysis)
outputs: metric verification table (GP-reported vs independently computed)
discrepancy report (any metric with > 50bps divergence)
NAV bridge reconciliation (line-by-line)
NAV mark analysis (implied cap rate vs market cap rate per asset)
reporting quality score card (timeliness, completeness, transparency, accuracy)
sub-line IRR adjustment (if applicable)

validationRules.description: Net IRR must be independently computed from LP cash flow data and compared to GP-reported.
NAV bridge must reconcile within 0.5% tolerance per line item.
Reporting quality must be scored across all 4 dimensions with overall grade.
Any metric divergence > 50bps must be flagged with root cause analysis.

inputs: LP total portfolio composition by asset class
LP investment policy statement (target allocations)
unfunded commitment inventory per GP
public market index data (for denominator effect modeling)
transaction-based RE index data (for lagged mark adjustment)
outputs: current RE allocation vs target with drift quantification
denominator effect scenario table (4 public equity decline scenarios)
over-commitment ratio (current and stressed)
unfunded commitment inventory with expected call timeline
secondary market assessment and rebalancing recommendation

validationRules.description: Current RE allocation must be computed as percentage of total portfolio.
At least 3 public equity decline scenarios must be modeled.
Over-commitment ratio must be computed with both current and stressed values.

inputs: capital account data for every GP relationship
distribution and capital call history (quarterly, trailing 12+)
unfunded commitments per fund
LP liquid reserve position
GP capital call forecasts (if available)
outputs: 20-quarter cash flow matching table with status per quarter
distribution reliability scores per fund
capital call exposure (unfunded by fund with expected pacing)
stress test results (4 scenarios with liquidity impact)
liquidity reserve assessment (ratio and classification)
secondary market valuation per fund interest

validationRules.description: Cash flow matching table must cover at least 12 quarters with net CF per quarter.
At least 3 stress scenarios must be modeled with reserve adequacy assessment.
Reserve ratio must be computed and classified (WELL_RESERVED through INADEQUATE).

inputs: GP ADV Part 1 and Part 2A
GP compliance manual and valuation policy
SOC 1 or SOC 2 report
fund administrator engagement details
auditor engagement details and prior audit findings
SEC EDGAR regulatory filings and enforcement history
outputs: ODD score card (back-office, valuation, conflicts, cyber, BCP, insurance, regulatory)
valuation methodology review with mark-to-exit analysis
conflict of interest map with mitigation assessment
operational risk matrix with aggregate score
top risks and remediation recommendations

validationRules.description: Aggregate ODD score must be computed from all 7 dimensions.
Valuation methodology must be reviewed and mark-to-exit consistency assessed.
All conflict categories must be assessed with mitigation status.
Overall classification must be INSTITUTIONAL_GRADE, ADEQUATE, BELOW_STANDARD, or UNACCEPTABLE.

inputs: GRESB Assessment results (3-year history if available)
GP TCFD report or climate risk disclosure
GP ESG policy document and annual ESG report
GP DEI data (leadership composition, metrics)
GRESB benchmark data for peer comparison
outputs: GRESB score decomposition with trajectory and peer comparison
TCFD quality assessment across all 4 pillars
DEI assessment with metrics and trajectory
ESG policy-implementation gap matrix
greenwashing risk assessment
ESG composite score and classification

validationRules.description: GRESB score must be decomposed and benchmarked, or GRESB non-participation must be flagged.
All 4 TCFD pillars must be scored, or absence of TCFD disclosure must be flagged.
ESG composite score must be computed with classification assigned.

inputs: all prior phase outputs (checkpoint data)
GP next-fund materials (PPM, term sheet, strategy update)
alternative manager data (track records, fees, terms)
market conditions and strategy outlook
LP portfolio context (allocation, liquidity, concentration)
outputs: 5-dimension scoring matrix with evidence and weights
composite score with override condition check
terminal verdict (RE-UP / REDUCE / EXIT) with conviction level (1-10)
opportunity cost analysis (current GP vs alternatives)
commitment sizing recommendation with adjustment rationale
IC memo draft (2-page investment committee recommendation)
negotiation points for successor fund (if RE-UP)
conditions under which verdict would change

validationRules.description: All 5 scoring dimensions must have scores (1-5) with supporting evidence.
Terminal verdict must be RE-UP, REDUCE, or EXIT with conviction level 1-10.
All override conditions must be evaluated and documented.
If verdict is RE-UP, recommended commitment amount must be specified with rationale.
IC memo draft must be produced with verdict, rationale, and key metrics.

inputs: GP performance data (per-fund net IRR, DPI, TVPI, deal-level MOIC)
vintage benchmark data (Cambridge Associates, Preqin, NCREIF)
peer fund performance data (strategy-matched, vintage-matched)
GP leverage data (fund-level and deal-level LTV)
market beta proxy data (NCREIF NPI, NCREIF ODCE)
outputs: peer group definition with criteria and fund count
vintage-adjusted return table with excess return per fund
percentile rankings (net IRR, DPI, TVPI per fund)
risk-adjusted metrics (Sharpe-like ratio, leverage-adjusted return, drawdown)
loss ratio comparison vs peer median and quartile
skill vs beta decomposition (market beta, manager alpha, alpha sources)
alpha persistence assessment across fund generations
definitive skill rating (1-5)

validationRules.description: Peer group must be defined with strategy, vintage, size, and geography criteria.
Returns must be vintage-adjusted with excess return computed per fund.
Market beta and manager alpha must be separately estimated.
Definitive skill rating must be assigned from SKILLED through UNSKILLED.

inbound.trigger: Quarterly fund data delivery or fee schedule update
inbound.action: Update LP performance tracking data and trigger Phase 3 quarterly review cycle

inbound.trigger: Portfolio allocation update or concentration analysis refresh
inbound.action: Update denominator effect analysis and portfolio fit assessment for re-up evaluation

outbound.trigger: Re-up decision finalized or terms feedback generated
outbound.action: Hand off re-up decision for GP communication and subscription processing

challengeLayer.activationCriteria: Terminal verdict determined or re-up recommendation finalized
